title: Tionese War

The '''Tionese War''', also known as the '''Tionese Wars''' or the '''Republic-Tionese wars''',<ref name="TEA" /> was a [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] war between both the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and [[Hutt Empire]] against the [[Honorable Union of Desevro and Tion]] from [[24,000 BBY|24,000]] until around [[23,900 BBY]]. The [[Tion Cluster/Legends|Tion Cluster]] had become a largely disunited backwater in the thousand years that had followed the defeat of [[Xim's empire]], and the Cluster's leaders saw a rapid conquest of the Republic and the acquisition of its technology as their salvation. The Union attacked the Republic without warning, and the Republic was unprepared for the Tionese invasion and suffered multiple defeats. However, its superior industrial might and the aid of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] allowed it to eventually subjugate [[Tionese (group)/Legends|Tionese]] space after a century-long conflict.

==Origins==
In the thousand years that had followed [[Xim/Legends|Xim the Despot's]] downfall in [[Third Battle of Vontor|the final battle]] of [[Xim Wars|his wars]] with the [[Hutt Empire]], [[Xim's empire|his empire]] had fragmented into a multitude of warring kingdoms. Living under the ever-present menace of the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]], the [[Tionese (group)/Legends|Tionese civilization]] had suffered technological and cultural stagnation. They had additionally experienced a devastating Hutt invasion of their colonies in the [[Ash Worlds/Legends|Kiirium Reaches]] when the Hutts had attempted to pre-empt a Tionese alliance with the growing Republic. This conflict, known as [[the Devouring]], rendered the Kiirium Reaches uninhabitable and transformed them into a desolate stretch of space that became known as the Ash Worlds.

In [[25,000 BBY/Legends|25,000 BBY]], a number of [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] unified to create the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Republic scouts moving rimward up the [[Perlemian Trade Route/Legends|Perlemian Trade Route]] soon encountered Tionese ships. Following the first contacts between Republic traders and Tionese aristocrats, the Tionese learned of both the Republic's superior technology and its lack of a powerful military. The rulers of [[Desevro/Legends|Desevro]] saw the Republic as a wealthy but weak target ripe for conquest, and persuaded the rest of the Tionese states to form a [[Honorable Union of Desevro and Tion|loose confederation]] with the goal of conquering the Republic.<ref name="TEGtW" />

The Republic ignored warnings from the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] (then based on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]]) about the build-up of Tionese war fleets that utilized reverse-engineered Republican technology, so the Republic was caught unaware when the Tionese launched their offensive.<ref name="TEGtW" />

==History==
[[File:The_Devouring_and_the_Tionese_War.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The Tionese War]]
The initial Tionese assault down the Perlemian Trade Route met little resistance. The Tionese seized [[Abhean/Legends|Abhean]], [[Roche asteroid field|Roche]], [[Lantillies/Legends|Lantillies]], and [[Tirahnn/Legends|Tirahnn]] before the Republic mounted a serious effort to construct its own war fleet. Tionese raiders destroyed the shipyards of Axum and [[Perlemia]] with a new weapon—[[pressure bomb]]s—before unleashing pressure bombs upon [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]] and [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].

After a last-ditch counterattack repelled the Tionese offensive, new Republican warships were launched from shipyards at [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]], [[Rendili/Legends|Rendili]] and [[Humbarine/Legends|Humbarine]]. Nearly a century of offensives and counteroffensives up and down the Perlemian followed. Republic agents managed to steer the Hutts into attacking the Tionese border, and eventually the Jedi joined the war, leading Republic forces in the hope of saving galactic civilization from sinking into barbarism.

With the Republic's overwhelming industrial might fully committed to the struggle, defeat for the Tion was only a matter of time. Believing that their culture would be annihilated, the Tionese resorted to sneak and suicide attacks, devastating even isolated colony [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] in [[Eucer sector/Legends|the Divide]]. One such attack on [[Okator VIII]] in the final months of the war left only [[Unidentified Republic soldier (Falang Minor)|one survivor]].<ref name="Author's Cut">{{SW|url=news/the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-4-the-rise-of-the-republic-needs-author-entry|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 4: The Rise of the Republic}}</ref>

These tactics served only to enrage the Republic, whose leaders embraced a doctrine of total war. Key Tionese worlds were pounded with pressure bombs, and eventually Desevro itself faced destruction under the guns of the Republic Navy. Desevro's attempt at unconditional surrender was ignored. The Republic's leaders were committed to sterilizing the planet in order to intimidate the rest of the Tion into surrendering. The Jedi Order strenuously objected to this, and in a series of tense meetings in orbit of Desevro, a compromise was reached where the Jedi would act as [[Jedi Watchman/Legends|Watchmen]] over the worlds of the Tion to ensure there would be no repeat of the war.

==Aftermath==
The war with the Republic was the Tion's swansong as a galactic power. The worlds of the now-defunct Union joined the Republic by approximately [[23,900 BBY]] as a member [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. In a few centuries, the Tion became simply another sector of the Republic.

Horrified by the sterilization of Desevro, the Jedi subsequently broke with the Republic, although the Order agreed to defend the Republic's frontier from their bases near the Tion, watching the cluster from [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress worlds]] like [[Falang Minor]].

Though the Republic was triumphant, its military's fanatical conduct of the war led the Core regions to regard a centralized military as a threat to the Republic's stability. Despite objections from both the Jedi and many Rimward systems, the standing military was downsized; the bulk of the Republic's military forces were reassigned to local sector governments. A centralized [[Republic Navy/Legends|federal navy]] would not be re-established until [[15,000 BBY/Legends|15,000 BBY]].
